req.title: Diagnostic report
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-012 mandates that DiagnosticReport resources must reference at least one Observation; missing or empty references must be rejected with HTTP 422. The test suite applies Equivalence Partitioning to partition valid vs. invalid reference states, Boundary Value Analysis to test empty and minimal-valid collections, and Negative Path testing to verify rejection behavior. State Transition Coverage ensures the report transitions from submitted to stored only when valid. All tests are deterministic, idempotent, and self-contained.

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observation_reference
preconditions: valid Observation with id obs-123 exists in system
actions: capture response status and Location header
trace: R-HLT-012: clinician issues report with valid Observation link
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observation_reference():
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "11502-2"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "11502-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/pat-001"},
        "result": [{"reference": "Observation/obs-123"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://localhost:8080/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer <clinician_token>"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert "id" in resp.json(), "Report id not in response"
    assert resp.json()["result"][0]["reference"] == "Observation/obs-123"

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_empty_observation_references
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with payload containing result=[] (empty array)
capture response status and error message
expected: error detail message indicates missing Observation references
trace: R-HLT-012: clinician issues report without Observation links
R-HLT-012: error pathway—422 when references empty
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_with_empty_observation_references():
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "11502-2"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "11502-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/pat-001"},
        "result": []
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://localhost:8080/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer <clinician_token>"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    error_msg = resp.json().get("issue", [{}])[0].get("details", {}).get("text", "")
    assert "observation" in error_msg.lower() or "reference" in error_msg.lower()

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_without_result_field
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with payload omitting result field entirely
expected: error indicates missing required result/observation references
trace: R-HLT-012: clinician issues report with no result field
R-HLT-012: error pathway—422 when references missing

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_single_observation_boundary
preconditions: valid Observation with id obs-boundary-1 exists
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with result array containing exactly one reference
capture response status and stored report id
expected: report successfully stored with single Observation reference
trace: R-HLT-012: boundary test—minimum valid reference count (1)
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_with_single_observation_boundary():
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "11502-2"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "11502-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/pat-001"},
        "result": [{"reference": "Observation/obs-boundary-1"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://localhost:8080/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer <clinician_token>"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert resp.json().get("id"), "Report id missing"
    assert len(resp.json()["result"]) == 1

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_multiple_observation_references
preconditions: valid Observations obs-m1, obs-m2, obs-m3 exist
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with result containing 3 observation references
capture response status and result count
expected: report stored with all three references intact
trace: R-HLT-012: clinician issues report with multiple valid Observation links

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_null_observation_references
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with result containing null value
expected: error indicates invalid or missing observation reference
trace: R-HLT-012: clinician issues report with null observation reference
R-HLT-012: error pathway—422 when references invalid
